title: ssh(by puttyng) session window can't automatically adjust window follow the window size
author: kc-roger
body_snippet:
![Snipaste_2022-03-10_18-25-33](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/33201372/157642922-89c9c663-8316-4644-b2f2-e88cfc170d3c.png)
As shown in the screenshot the window of putty's window session does not automatically adjust to fill the entire window
![Snipaste_2022-03-10_18-28-35](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/33201372/157643597-4158a142-5851-4aa6-8c4f-a3b317e3e174.png)
I have upgraded to the latest PuttyNG.

iterations.description: The SSH session window (PuTTYNG) does not automatically adjust its size when the parent mRemoteNG window is resized, requiring an update to the process control and window management.

comments.author: simonai1254
comments.snippet: What Version of mRemoteNG, PuttyNG and Windows are you running? I don't have this issue on my machine...

comments.author: kc-roger
comments.snippet:
> What Version of mRemoteNG, PuttyNG and Windows are you running? I don't have this issue on my machine...

mRemoteNG 1.76.20, PuttyNG 0.76.0, Windows 10 21H1

comments.author: Kvarkas
comments.snippet: its related hi resolution monitor I think, @kc-roger could you confirm resolution and monitor settings (zoom, dpi etc)

comments.author: kc-roger
comments.snippet:
> its related hi resolution monitor I think, @kc-roger could you confirm resolution and monitor settings (zoom, dpi etc)

This problem did not start at the beginning, but became so after changing the putty default setting(connection-seconds between keepalives to 0) and saving it, and then reestablishing the session.
![Snipaste_2022-03-11_08-41-48](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/33201372/157779761-7cb77125-e201-43e7-bb1d-db86fb4243e9.png)

_Automated by mRemoteNG Issue Intelligence System_

notes:
AI triage (gemini): The SSH session window (PuTTYNG) does not automatically adjust its size when the parent mRemoteNG window is resized, requiring an update to the process control and window management.
Approach: Add a method to `ProcessController.cs` to resize the external PuTTYNG window using P/Invoke (SetWindowPos/MoveWindow from `NativeMethods.cs`). Override the resize event in the relevant mRemoteNG UI window (e.g., `BaseWindow` descendant) to get the new dimensions and pass them to the `ProcessController`'s resize method.
